text:
(84) 
The Claim That Shaykh Al-Albani Was Overly Lenient and Shaykh Muqbil Overly Strict in Hadith Grading

Question: 
It is said about Shaykh Nasir Ad-Din Al-Albani that he was lenient in his authentication and weakening of hadiths, and about Shaykh Muqbil that he was overly severe. How true are these statements?

Sheikh Yahya Al-Hajoori
As for the claim that Shaykh Al-Albani was lenient in authentication and weakeningthis is incorrect. How could that be? He never weakened a hadith without providing clear evidence for its weakness. He was extremely meticulous in weakening hadiths. His book Silsilah Adh-Daifah (The Chain of Weak Hadiths) stands as a testament to this, showing his great precision. Regarding authentication, he would sometimes exercise his own reasoning (ijtihad) on certain hadiths, citing various chains and applying his own methodological principles. He might say, "This hadith, through these chains, is hasan in my view." This is similar to what Al-Hafidh Ibn Hajar does in Bulugh Al-Maram, where he says, "This hadith is hasan," even though some other hadith masters or researchers may disagree. The same can be said for Az-Zaylai and other scholarsthis is a normal matter of scholarly difference.

It is true that some hadiths in Silsilah As-Sahihah (The Chain of Authentic Hadiths) which he deemed authentic are not accepted by some other scholars. However, in reality, his ijtihad was very strong, and he provided the Muslim Ummah with an immense treasure that neither a scholar nor a student can do without. He remains a mujtahid, like other mujtahids in the field of hadith. A student of knowledge should still examine the hadiths, even those found in Silsilah As-Sahihah or Irwa Al-Ghalil, and form their own conclusion based on what they see, even if it aligns with Shaykh Al-Albanis ruling. If a student feels they have not reached the level to judge a hadith as hasan or sahih as the Shaykh did, they can simply hold their own informed opinion. And all praise is due to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

As for the claim that Shaykh Muqbil was severe in authentication and weakeningthere is no doubt that he was extremely meticulous. The "severity" here simply refers to his rigorous precision. Shaykh Muqbil would scrutinise many hadiths in light of hidden defects (ilal). I once asked him directly: "O Shaykh, the hadiths you included in As-Sahih Al-Musnad, did you examine them for hidden defects?" He replied, "I mostly examine them for hidden defects." He continued this practice throughout his life, constantly reviewing As-Sahih Al-Musnad against books of ilal. If Al-Hafidh Ad-Daraqutni, Abu Hatim, Abu Zurah, Al-Hafidh Ibn Hajar, or any of the hadith masters stated that a hadith had a defect, Shaykh Muqbil would either remove it from his collection or keep it with a note indicating its defect.

In my observation, the criticisms regarding authentication directed at our Shaykh (Muqbil), may Allah have mercy on him, are far fewer than those directed at Shaykh Al-Albani. This is worth noting, considering the vast number of hadiths that Shaykh Al-Albani ruled upon compared to the number that our Shaykh ruled upon.

If Al-Hafidh Ad-Daraqutni, Abu Hatim, Abu Zurah, Al-Hafidh Ibn Hajar, or any of the hadith masters stated that a hadith had a defect, Shaykh Muqbil would either remove it from his collection or keep it with a note indicating its defect. In my observation, the criticisms regarding authentication directed at our Shaykh (Muqbil), may Allah have mercy on him, are far fewer than those directed at Shaykh Al-Albani. This is worth noting, considering the vast number of hadiths that Shaykh Al-Albani ruled upon compared to the number that our Shaykh ruled upon.

text:
"" Ash-Shaikh al-Allmah, Slih al-Fawzn (may Allh preserve him): 

Advice to students of knowledge who busy themselves with Takfr, Tabd and seeking out the slips of the scholars and the Salafi callers to Allh, claiming that this is the path of Shaikh al-Fawzn and others from the people of knowledge. 

A summary of the Shaikh al-Fawzns speech: 

This is a trial, and not permissible: busying oneself with the mistakes of the people, and causing people to flee from the scholars, the students of knowledge, and from the callers to Allh. This is a fitnahit is not permissible to act upon that, nor to follow the one who does that. 

Rather, it is obligatory for there to be mutual advisingthe Religion is sincere adviceand cooperation upon righteousness and piety between the Muslims, and to act with knowledge. 

As for busying oneself with this person and that person, that is backbiting and repellingand backbiting is among the major sinsand it does not rectify anything; instead, it corrupts and splits. 

If we see something in a person, we advise him, either by writing to him or by speaking to him. As for sitting and backbiting, that is harm. This leads to the splitting of the Muslims and causes people to flee from seeking knowledge. This behaviour is not permissible at all. 

They are lying, those who say that this is our way, or the way of others [from the scholars]indeed, we warn against this in our lectures, our books, and we warn against this path.

Recorded about 12 years ago. And remind, for verily, the reminding benefits the believers. (Adh-Dhariyat: 55)"
"
--------------

Abu khadeejah talking about unfair tabdee is like AMJ talking about the khawarij.

Abu khadeejah literally makes tabdee on the vast majority of salafi scholarship.

text:
Sincerity

Abu Bakr at-Tutl (d.270H) said:

Know that among the foremost things upon a servant is that he make all of his deeds purely for Allah. Much can be said on this topic, but its foundation, sincerity in ones actions, is this:

That you do a deed seeking only Allah by it, and you do not like that anyone among people should come to know of it. So if someone does happen to see your deed, you dislike that in your heart; you are not pleased by it, and you do not want anyone to praise you for anything you have done. Nor do you use it to gain status or standing with them. This is the foundation of sincerity in action, Allah willing and Allah is the One whose help is sought.

[at-Tutl, Kitb az-Zuhd]

text:
"It these quburists, who claim to be from Ahl al-Sunna, read some books they would realize that it was Allh who revived the dead after Eis (peace be upon him) prayed to Allh for it.

Imm al-abar said:

Eis (peace be upon him) revived the deceased by praying to Allh for them; thus, his prayer was answered.

| Tafsr al-abar, vol. 5, p. 424.

Even the Ashar scholar, Fakhr al-Deen al-Rz, said:

Is it permissible to say that He (Allh the Most High) placed within the self of Eis (peace be upon him) a characteristic in the sense that whenever he blew into something his blowing would necessitate for that thing to become alive? Or to say that it isnt like that; but rather, Allh, the Most High, used to create life within that body with His ability at the moment when Eis (peace be upon him) blew into it as a way of manifesting a miracle? This second explanation is the truth because Allh, the Most High, said: The One who created death and life (67:2). And it was mentioned that Ibrhm (peace be upon him) said in his debate with the king: My Lord is the One who gives life and cause death (2:258), so if this characteristic occurred for other than Him then that argument would have perished.

| al-Tafsr al-Kabr, by Fakhr al-Deen al-Rz, vol. 8, pp. 228 229.

Its essentially the same as the story mentioned in the following verse:

So We said: "Strike him (the dead man) with a piece of it (the cow)." Thus Allh brings the dead to life and shows you HisAyt(proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) so that you may understand.

| Qurn [Hilali & Khan Translation], ch. 2, v. 73.

In this verse, it is Allh who revived the dead man at the moment when he was struck by a piece of the cow. It wasnt the piece from the cow that revived him.

           "

text:
"**Shaykh Abd al-Rahman al-Barrak on UK Student Loans**

The Shaykh was asked about student loans in the UK with these features:

 Issued by the government to support students  
 Interest is applied from the moment the loan is taken  
 Repayment starts after graduation and employment, if income reaches a threshold  
 If income is low, repayment is not required  
 The loan may be written off after 30 years, or due to death or inability to work  

Verdict from the Shaykh:

It is not permissible to take this loan.

Reason:

 It involves interest paid by the borrower  
 It is a complicated contract with many conditions and issues  

Even with income-based repayment and possible write-off, the presence of interest means it should be avoided entirely."

text:
"Every Muslim who is being slandered by the hypocrites/disbelievers should be defended without a doubt, or offered a word of support at a minimum (even if they dont need it). This is the bare minimum rights that any of the Mashayikh, Mujahideen, or the laymen Muslims have upon their Muslim brothers.

That being said, do not be of those who voluntarily bring heartache or sadness to Muslims, by sharing with them slander against them, that they might otherwise be ignorant of occurring. Even the Prophet     was hurt by the slander/Kufr of Quraysh, Allah   Says:

      

Imam Ibn Jarir At-Tabari   comments on this Ayah and said:

The Exalted says  in His mention  to His Prophet Muhammad : And indeed We know, O Muhammad, that your chest is constricted by what these polytheists of your people say  by their denial of you, their mockery of you, and their mockery of what you have brought to them.

Its also mentioned by Ibn Abee ad-Dunya in his Al-Ishraf:

tim ibn Yay narrated to us, saying: I heard Muammad ibn Yay al-Marwaz say: A man said to Wahb ibn Munabbih, So-and-so has insulted you. He replied: Could the devil find no messenger other than you?

And these are widely cited although I couldnt find a Sanad for them, although due to its meaning, it is worthy of mentioning as a reminder for us all, InshaAllah:

Its said that a man came to a wise man and said: So-and-so has insulted you. He replied: He shot an arrow at me and it did not reach me  so why did you carry the arrow and lodge it in my heart?

And its said that a man came to al-Shfi  may Allah have mercy on him  and said: So-and-so speaks ill of you. He replied: If you are telling the truth, then you are a tale-carrier (Nammm); and if you are lying, then you are a transgressor. So the man was ashamed and left.

Defend your brothers in Islam, without hesitation, but be weary not fall into the trap of Nameemah unintentionally, lest you hurt your brother(s) unintentionally. If anything, if someone gets slandered often, be the bearer of good and positive news instead. Work to spread their Dawah, and make it widespread. Share with them the messages of support that flood social media during the slander, instead of the slander itself. 

Theres a wonderful website  thats been assembled, Allahumma Brek. Every time someone slanders the Shaykh, share this website. Let the hypocrites die in their rage."

text:
"When a Scholar Makes an Error, the Whole World Makes an Error!

Sh Bad-ud-dn writes in the Muqaddimah of his Tafsr after explaining that requests were made of him to author a tafsr:

"So I began to ponder over the affair, and consider what I had to offer and the reality of the task at hand. If they had entrusted me to move a towering mountain, it would have been easier for me. I took a step forward and back again.

Another consideration: man is prone to making mistakes and forgetting, and I feared that I would make a mistake or an error, for when a scholar makes an error, the whole world makes an error, to the point that I intended to excuse myself."

We cannot fathom the weight on a scholar's shoulders. This great imm, with a level of knowledge acknowledged by the likes of Al-Albni, Muqbil, Rab, would not start writing his Tafsr because he feared making mistakes."

text:
""Why do people encourage people to speak about things, even if they may have no knowledge about it? If you want to make a difference, you dont make the whole world speak. You ask the people with knowledge to speak"
"
--------------

This brother made a very good point .
Most of us dont even know the entirety of juz Amma , what makes you think you can discuss the differences between Ahkaam and aqidah ?

It is known from our pious predecessors that the foundation of gaining ilm was to memorise the entire Quran before studying rullings , ilmul Hadith , jarh wa tadeel etc  . So if you cannot do that then stay silent until you have done so .

Theres a famous story between imam Abu hanifah and Imam Muhammad Bin Hassan Ash-Shaybaani. 
Ash-Shaybaani wanted to enter and sit with Abu Hanifah during his duroos . But Abu Hanfiah had stipulated a condition and that was to first go memorise the Quran and then come back once he had completed . 
Imam Muhammad was so eager that he memorised the words of Allah  entirely in just 8 days .

So, if you want to succeed as a student of knowledge, do Hifz Ul-Quran , then continue with your further studies . You cannot master one and leave the other out , they both go hand in hand . 

May Allah grant us to be great Huffz and allow the words of Allah to enter our hearts

"A voice called out:

This is how the funerals of the Imms of Ahl al-Sunnah should be!

The crowd burst into tears at these words. People threw their handkerchiefs and turbans onto the wagon carrying the Shaykhs body. Over 200,000 men where estimated to be in attendance. 

Salt al-Ghib (funeral prayer in absentia) was preformed for him in most regions of the Muslim world, near and far. Some travellers reported that the news of his death had even reached the remotest areas of China so much so a public call was made for the funeral prayer in these places: 

The prayer for the interpreter of the Qurn

- Abridged from Ibn Rajabs account of Ibn Taymiyyahs death, from his work: Dhayl Dabaqt al-Hanbilah.",

text:
"A questioner asks: What is the ruling on offering congratulations and gifts on the occasion of Valentines Day, which falls in mid-February each year?

All praise is due to Allah, and peace and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allah and upon his family and companions. As for what follows:

This holiday is a festival of the kuffar and Mushrikeen. Even if it is called Valentines Day, it is a festival claimed by the Greeks, an ancient festival for a deity they worshipped besides Allah. 

Whether this holiday was for a deity they worshipped or merely for what they call and claim to be love, then congratulating others on it, celebrating this day, and honoring it is forbidden and not permissible.

We, the people of Islam, have only two Eids: Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha. It is not permissible to congratulate others for any Eid besides these two. So congratulating others on this day and honoring it is not allowed; it is forbidden. You are not permitted to congratulate anyone on it, nor are you permitted to accept congratulations from anyone on this day. Rather, it is obligatory upon you and every Muslim to reject anyone who congratulates you on this day and to clarify to people that congratulating others on this day is forbidden and not permissible, and that it is a form of imitating the Kuffar and Mushrikeen in their false festivals, which are based on falsehood, disbelief, immorality, and wickedness.

How can they claim it is a day of love and magnify love while they are among the most hostile people toward Islam and Muslims, waging war against the religion of Allah and against the people of Islam? A Muslim should not be deceived by their labels or slogans. 

These Kuffar and wicked people are among the furthest from what is called love. They are the ones killing us, killing our brothers in Syria, Iraq, and everywhere, yet they claim this day is Valentines Day.

Therefore, it is not permissible to congratulate others on this day, nor is it permissible for a Muslim to honor it, give gifts on it, or accept gifts on it. It is a forbidden day; honoring it, celebrating it, and offering congratulations for such false festivals is forbidden.

We have only two Eids: Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha, and these are the ones in which it is permissible to rejoice.

Likewise, commercial shops that sell gifts such as flowers, teddy bears, and similar items on this day, some people even change their pages or displays to red or use symbols associated with celebrating this day, selling flowers or anything that assists in honoring this day is not permissible. It is forbidden and is considered helping in falsehood. 

Allah says: Cooperate in righteousness and piety, and do not cooperate in sin and transgression.

Whoever sells such flowers with the intention of honoring that day, his sale is forbidden, and the money he earns from it is unlawful. Every body nourished by unlawful earnings, the Fire is more deserving of it. So it is not permissible to take such money, nor to sell flowers, change displays, alter slogans, or use symbols such as roses or the color red for this day, for these are forbidden and reprehensible matters.

It is obligatory upon a Muslim to honor the symbols of Allah, to honor the religion of Allah, to show disavowal of these Kuffar and wicked people, and to distinguish himself from them in their festivals and their false, misguided beliefs. For resemblance in outward matters leads to resemblance in inward matters.

We ask Allah for well-being and safety. 

Shaykh Khalid al Fulaij (may Allah free him)"

text:
4. Shaking hands with non-Mahram (unrelated) women: This is an affliction that has become widespread, and no one has escaped it except those whom Allah has shown mercy. It is prohibited due to what was narrated by Abdullah bin Al-Shikhkhir who said: I heard Ma'qil bin Yasar say: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (For one of you to be stabbed in the head with an iron needle is better for him than to touch a woman who is not permissible for him.)  Narrated by Al-Tabarani in Al-Mu'jam Al-Kabeer (486).
Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And in the hadith, there is a severe threat for the one who touches a woman who is not permissible for him; thus, it contains evidence for the prohibition of shaking hands with women, because that is undoubtedly included in touching.)  As-Silsilah As-Saheehah (1/448).
> 
> (So this hadith indicates explicitly the prohibition of touching a non-Mahram woman, and from this is: shaking hands; as the Lawgiver has attached a severe threat to touching a woman who is not permissible for him, which is: being stabbed with an iron needle. Attaching a severe threat to an action indicates its prohibition.)  Al-Musarahah fi Ahkam Al-Musafahah (p.62).
> 
5. Free mixing of men and women: This often occurs in parks and recreational areas, and there is much evidence regarding the prohibition of the free mixing of men and women, so the Muslim must be wary of that. From Abu Usaid Malik bin Rabi'ah, may Allah be pleased with him, that he heard the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, saywhile he was coming out of the mosque, and men and women had mixed in the street: (Draw back, for you must not walk in the middle of the street; keep to the sides of the street. So women used to cling to the wall so much that their garments would catch on the wall due to their clinging to it.)  Narrated by Abu Dawud (5272).
Sheikh Muhammad bin Ibrahim, the former Grand Mufti of the Saudi Lands, said:
> (And the point of evidence is: that if the Messenger of Allahpeace and blessings be upon him and his familyprevented mixing in the street because it leads to temptation, then how can it be said that mixing is permissible elsewhere?!)  Majmoo Fatawa wa Rasa'il (10/42).
> 
Ali bin Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him, said: (Do you not feel ashamed or have jealousy? For it has reached me that your women go out into the markets, crowding the 'Ulooj (1).)  Narrated by Ahmad (1118).
(1) Al-'Ulooj: Plural of 'Ilj, which refers to a disbelieving man of non-Arab descent.
Sheikh Al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And it was from the Sunnah of the Prophetpeace and blessings be upon him and his familyand the Sunnah of his successors to separate men and women... because the mixing of one group with the other is a cause for temptation; for when men mix with women, it is like mixing fire and wood.)  Al-Istiqamah (1/360).
> 
6. Women leaving their homes perfumed and scented: This action is not permissible; rather, it has come in the hadith that whoever does this is a fornicator. From Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari who said: The Messenger of Allah - peace and blessings be upon him - said: (Any woman who puts on perfume and passes by a people so they can smell her fragrance, is a fornicator.)  Narrated by An-Nasa'i (9361), and Ibn Khuzaymah (1681), and Al-Albani said: Its chain of narration is Hasan (good).
Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (Al-Haytami mentioned in 'Az-Zawajir' 2/37 that a woman leaving her home perfumed and adorned is among the major sins, even if her husband permitted her.)  Jilbab Al-Mar'ah Al-Muslimah (p.139).
>

text:
"Shaykh li al-Fawzn speaks about those who allow the disbelievers into the lands of muslims when assisting them against the muslims and how this is disbelief even if he hates their religion.

Interestingly, he then speaks about those who bring them in and enabled them to build churches and the likes, and this is what he says :

_

Shaykh Sulaymn ibn Abd Allh ibn Muammad ibn Abd al Wahhb said: [That if someone openly displays their] agreement with their religion [of the Mushrikin]

Shaykh li al-Fawzn said: Meaning: He enabled them [the mushrikin] to establish churches in the lands of the Muslims, and granted them freedom, and said to the Muslims: Do not prevent them from anything; whatever they request from you, comply with it. This is mudhanah, because our religion forbids this.



Shaykh Sulaymn ibn Abd Allh ibn Muammad ibn Abd al-Wahhb said: [And if he does this then] indeed he is a disbeliever like them.

Shaykh li al-Fawzn commentates: Because he enabled the [religion of the] disbelievers and was pleased with their religion, and he forewent what the religion of Islm obliged upon him  namely disassociating the religion of the disbelievers and disavowing it. He did not disavow it; rather, he permitted it, justified it, and granted them freedom [to establish their religion] in the lands of the Muslims.

Shaykh Sulaymn ibn Abd Allh ibn Muammad ibn Abd al-Wahhb said: [And if he does this then indeed he is a disbeliever like them] even if he detests their religion and hates them.

Shaykh li al-Fawzn commentates: Meaning: if he loves their religion, then this makes him a disbeliever (kfir), an apostate, if he establish them. And if he establishes them while he hates their religion, then this act of his constitutes apostasy from Islm, because he has assisted them against the Muslims.

        (31,32)",

text:
""A Concise Summary on the Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid 

Sheikh Saleem Al-Hilli afidhahullh states:

All praise is due to Allh, Lord of the Worlds, and peace and blessings be upon the Master of the Messengers, Muammad, and upon his family, companions, and those who follow them with excellence until the Day of Judgment.

My dear brothers and sisters...There has been much discussion regarding this matter (i.e. Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid). Some have asked: Does one prayer suffice for the other, or is it obligatory for a Muslim to perform both the Eid and Jumuah prayers?

Many who have spoken on this issue have done so without the requisite qualification to understand the evidences transmitted from the Messenger of Allh . This is because they approach the matter based on a flawed methodology: they first adopt a belief, then seek out supporting evidence. This is a mistake! The correct methodology is to derive beliefs from evidence, not to impose ones beliefs or schools of thought upon the evidence.

Therefore, I shall briefly present the adths and reports attributed to the Messenger of Allh  and the noble Companionsmay Allh be pleased with themthen explain the positions of the recognised schools of Fiqh on this issue, relying upon Allh Almighty.

So I say, with Allhs guidance, that there are both prophetic traditions (marf) and reports from the companion (mawqf) on this matter.

The first is the adth of Zayd ibn Arqam (may Allh be pleased with him), in which Muwiyah ibn Ab Sufyn (may Allh be pleased with him) asked him: Did you witness the Messenger of Allh  having two Eids occur on the same day? He replied, Yes. He asked, What did he do? Zayd said, He performed the Eid prayer, then granted a concession regarding the Jumuah prayer, saying: Whoever wishes to pray, let him pray. This adth was narrated by Amad, Ab Dwd, An-Nas, Ibn Mjah, Ad-Drim, and Al-Hkim, and it is authentic.

The second adth is from Ab Hurayrah (may Allh be pleased with him), in which the Messenger of Allh  said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we will indeed perform the Jumuah. This adth was also narrated by Ab Dwd and Ibn Mjah, and it is authentic.

The third adth is from Ibn Umar (may Allh be pleased with him), who said: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh . He led the people in prayer, then said: Whoever wishes to attend the Jumuah may do so, and whoever wishes to refrain may do so. This adth was narrated by Ibn Mjah. A-abarn also transmitted it with the wording: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh  on the day of Eid Al-Fir and Friday. The Messenger of Allh  led them in the Eid prayer, then turned to face them and said: O people, you have achieved good and reward. Indeed, we are holding the Jumuah. Whoever wishes to join us may do so, and whoever wishes to return to his family may do so. This narration is also authentic.

The fourth adth is from Ibn Abbs (may Allh be pleased with them both), in which the Messenger of Allh  said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we are gathering, in-sh-Allh. This adth is also authentic and narrated by Ibn Mjah.

From the reports of the Companions: A ibn Ab Rab said: Ibn Az-Zubayr led us in the Eid prayer on a Friday early in the day. Then we proceeded to attend Jumuah, but he did not come out to us, so we prayed individually. Ibn Abbs was in A-if, and when he returned and we mentioned this to him, he said: He acted in accordance with the Sunnah. This was reported by Ab Dwd, and by Ibn Khuzaymah with a different wording and with an addition: Ibn Az-Zubayr said: I saw Umar ibn Al-Khab, when two Eids coincided, do the same."
"
--------------

I would like to mention the post below by Shaykh Salim al-Hilali   is amazing as he mentions many evidences and keeps it concise. But people are lazy they would rather make jokes on social media about Jumuah and Eid being the same day rather than reading.

Here is also Sh Sulayman ar-Ruhayli   speaking about it

text:
"A Note Regarding my Trip to the USA 8th - 13th April, 2026

It has once again been a truly enjoyable visit to the USA. I extend my sincere thanks to our beloved communities in Roanoke, Virginia, and Columbus, Ohio, for organizing and facilitating the events over the past few days. I am deeply grateful for their outstanding hospitality.

By the blessing of Allh, I had the opportunity to meet communities being cultivated upon the way of the Scholarshonouring them, loving them, and recognizing their rightful status. May Allh continue to grant them success.

The dawah in the USA is clearly growing. I ask Allh to bless the communities of Roanoke and Columbus, and all Salaf communities, as well as all our respected and noble brothersthe honourable dut and teachers in the USA who strive tirelessly to benefit the Muslims across the country. They teach the Quran and Sunnah, connect the people to the Scholars, and nurture within them love and respect for them: Ab Muhammad al-Maghrab, Abul Hasan Mlik, Hasan Somali, Abul-Abbs Ms Richardson, Anwar Wright, Umar Quinn, Rasheed Barbee, Raha Batts, Jaml Finch, Hamza AbdurRazzq, Mustaf George, Ab Ansa Abdul Hakeem, and others among the teachers upon the same commendable path whom I may have missed.

For decades, our dawah in the West has been firmly built upon returning to the Scholarsmaintaining strong ties with them, seeking their guidance in our affairs, and recognizing our position beneath them. By the permission of Allh, if we remain steadfast upon this path, we will continue to grow and progress, our dawah will only become stronger, and we will find the sweetness of brotherhood, love and unity between us.

As for those who are no longer able to uphold this way, and have instead opened doors to corruption and misplaced boldness against the scholars that we long worked within the dawah to keep firmly shut for yearsand in fact rebuked opposers who often attempt to open themthen do not concern yourselves with such individuals or their supporters issuing lengthy and meandering justifications for their weak and erroneous positions lacking resolveat a time when nothing but firm resolve itself is the requirement.

Ab Muadh Taqweem

25th Shawwl 1447h
13th April, 2026

  "

"Imam Jamil Al-Amin passed away today while still confined within the prison walls. Like his companion, Imam Luqman Abdullah, who preceded him, these were men who, in shAllh, remained steadfast and true to their covenant with Allh. They stood in stark contrast to many other imams in the West, who faltered and compromised.

Though they may not have been formally recognized as ulama, theyin shAllhacted upon the knowledge they possessed, which is what truly matters.         . Even amid his suffering, Imam Jamil remained thoughtful, conveying his salm to me through some who were in touch with him.

May Allh forgive them both, envelop them in His infinite mercy, illuminate their graves with radiant light, and elevate their ranks among the righteous.

Ahmad Musa Jibril 
2 Jumda ath-Thn 1447",93.0

"Three Duas to recite in every night in Ramadan

1. Dua for Forgiveness (Especially for Laylatul Qadr)

      

Allahumma innaka afuwwun tuhibbul afwa fafu anni

O Allah, You are Most Forgiving, and You love forgiveness, so forgive me.

2. Dua for Mercy and Guidance

           

Rabbana la tuzigh quloobana bada idh hadaytana wahab lana min ladunka rahmah

Our Lord, do not let our hearts deviate after You have guided us, and grant us mercy from Yourself.

3. Dua for Acceptance of Worship

      

Rabbana taqabbal minna innaka Antas-Sameeul-Aleem

Our Lord, accept from us. Indeed, You are the All-Hearing, All-Knowing."

"I dont like to comment on spubs minions too much, since it can be likened to abusing the mentally handicapped, but some instances its a must to highlight just how illiterate they are. This Zindq Madkhali is attempting to refute a brother for allegedly misrepresenting, only to misrepresent it himself and refute himself. This is standard Madkhali refutation protocol. Copy and paste, cringe flex on social media, and hope the audience is just as illiterate as him.

This idiot fails to read the two words before the highlighted word. Which say:     meaning, Even if he doesnt believe [that this system of governance] is better than the governance of the Shariah].

This brain dead kid saw the word  and thought the author is conditioning believing a certain system of governance is better than the Shariah, for it to be Major Kufr, when in fact the author does the opposite. Lol, Miskeen. The real kicker for this illiterate kid, and perhaps the pen is lifted from him  is when you read the statement as a whole, when the author (Ibn Baz) is describing nullifiers of Islam:

      (1)                (2)                                       .

And included in that also is everyone who believes that it is permissible to govern by other than the Sharah of Allah in transactions, or prescribed punishments, or other than them  Even if he doesnt believe that [this system of governance] is better than the governance of the Shariah  because by that he would have permitted what Allah has forbidden by consensus. And everyone who deems permissible what Allah has forbidden  from that which is known of the religion by necessity, such as fornication, wine, usury, and judging [between the people] by other than The Sharah of Allah  then he is a disbeliever by the consensus of the Muslims.

Perhaps the Madkhali will persist in his idiocy and illiteracy, because they notice the word  again here at the beginning. The Madkhali trope is to say that  in this passage [and others] can only mean  with respect to the Shariah of Allah. The response to this only requires 3rd grade reading comprehension.

When the author first uses it, its (1)  , and when he uses it later on (2) its referring to  , and the proof for that, is he distinguishes between two different shariahs by using them to contrast one another.

The Madkhali reading of the text, and I use reading very loosely here: 

Its Kufr only if someone believes its allowed within the Shariah, to govern by other than the Shariah even if they dont believe their [innovated] shariah is better than the Shariah of Allah.

How can you ascribe something to the Shariah, yet at the same time distinguish the same thing you attributed to the Shariah, to another shariah?! The statement doesnt even make sense if read this way.

The reality is, the author distinguishes between two shariahs, so by basic reading comprehension, the use of  cant be the same for both instances, because if so, the latter use would contradict its former use.  Rather what is obvious and clearly the intent, is that the latter use of  qualifies the use of the former .

In this statement alone, the author highlights the mere action of [permanently] governing by other shariahs as Kufr, even if only in some matters, and that such an action is Kufr by consensus. He also highlights how scholars have used , which will be highlighted in a future video InshaAllah.

Madkhalis are by far the most intellectually handicapped of the claimants of Islam, only rivaled by Jahmis (modern-day Asharis)."

text:
"Ali ibn al-Ja'd narrated to us: Mahbb az-Zhid nformed: al-Hasan [al-Basri] said:

Indeed, I encountered people (Sahba) for whom a garment was never folded (i.e. stored away carefully or possessed in excess), nor did any one of them ever crave a desire upon his family (for worldly pleasures), nor did any of them ever order (his family) to prepare food (for himself) , nor did any one of them ever divide an inheritance with his brother. Indeed, one of them would have an inheritance share between him and his brother and he would say: 'It is yours.' He did not love to occupy himself with anything from worldly affairs. text:
We have until the end of Ramadan.

1,000,000 needs to be raised to secure Markaz us-Sunnah permanently. If this amount is reached, the building is signed over. The remaining balance will be paid in instalments, in sh Allh.

The Messenger of Allah  said:
*When a person dies, his deeds come to an end except for three: ongoing charity, beneficial knowledge, or a righteous child who makes dua for him.*
(Reported by Muslim)

This is a clear opportunity for ongoing charity.

If you are able to give, give now.
If you have already given, may Allah accept it and multiply it.
If you cannot give, please share it.

Ramadan passes. Deadlines do not move.

BarakAllahu feekum to everyone supporting, and may Allah place barakah in every contribution, Ameen.
